expectations.text: Recommends `winml inspect` as the first, cheapest check (config-only).
expectations.evidence: Step 1 winml inspect; 'reads only the model config (no weights, no GPU).'

expectations.text: Identifies OpenVINO as the EP for Intel NPU.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'For your Intel NPU, the EP is OpenVINO (--ep ov, --device npu).'

expectations.text: Explains analyze's role: linter that classifies operators as supported/partial/unsupported per EP.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'analyze runs a static lint of every operator in the exported ONNX against the target EP and tells you which ops are supported / partial / unsupported.'

expectations.text: Does NOT just say 'build the full pipeline and see what happens'.
expectations.evidence: Explicit section 'Why not just run winml build and let it fail fast?' justifying the pre-flight.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: All ? winml commands verified runnable against --help.
